message:
Implement quality gates framework (bd-ldyv)

Add SECTION 13.7 with quality gates functions that run before push:

Auto-detection:
- detect_test_command: Finds test command for Makefile, npm, Cargo, Python, Go, shell
- detect_lint_command: Finds linter for npm, shellcheck, ruff/flake8, go vet

Gate runners:
- run_test_gate: Executes tests with timeout, returns JSON result
- run_lint_gate: Executes linter, returns JSON result
- run_secret_scan: Uses gitleaks or regex fallback for secret detection

Integration:
- run_quality_gates: Orchestrates all gates, integrates with policy config
- update_plan_with_gates: Updates review-plan.json with gate results

Features:
- Respects per-repo policy for custom test/lint commands
- Captures output summaries for diagnostics
- Returns structured JSON for automation
- Uses exit codes 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=warning

Unblocks bd-vcr9 (gh_actions execution).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
  
  "metadata": {
    "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
    "duration_seconds": 900,
    "context_usage_percent": 45,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
    "driver": "local"
  }
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top Level
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| schema_version | int | yes | Always 1 for this version |
| run_id | string | yes | Unique review run identifier |
| repo | string | yes | owner/repo format |
| worktree_path | string | yes | Absolute path to worktree |
| items | array | yes | Work items reviewed |
| questions | array | no | Questions asked/answered |
| git | object | no | Git state (may be empty if no changes) |
| gh_actions | array | no | GitHub actions to perform in Apply |
| metadata | object | yes | Session metadata |

### Item Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| type | "issue"\|"pr" | yes | Work item type |
| number | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
| title | string | yes | Item title |
| priority | string | yes | high/normal/low |
| decision | string | yes | fix/skip/needs-info/closed |
| notes | string | no | Explanation of decision |
| risk_level | string | no | low/medium/high/n/a |
| files_changed | array | no | Files modified for this item |
| lines_changed | int | no | Net lines changed |

### Question Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | string | yes | Unique question ID |
| prompt | string | yes | Question text |
| options | array | no | Available options |
| recommended | string | no | Agent's recommendation |
| answered | bool | yes | Whether answered |
| answer | string | no | Selected answer (if answered) |
| answered_at | string | no | ISO timestamp |

### gh_action Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| op | string | yes | comment/close/label/merge |
| target | string | yes | issue#N or pr#N |
| body | string | no | Comment body (for comment op) |
| reason | string | no | Close reason (for close op) |
| labels | array | no | Labels to add (for label op) |

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

## Plan Summary
```bash
summarize_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    jq -r '
        "Repository: \(.repo)",
        "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
        "  - Fixed: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length)",
        "  - Skipped: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length)",
        "  - Needs info: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length)",
        "Commits: \(.git.commits // [] | length)",
        "Tests: \(if .git.tests.ok then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
        "gh_actions pending: \(.gh_actions // [] | length)"
    ' "$plan_file"
}
```

## Testing
- Validate correct plan passes
- Validate missing fields rejected
- Validate invalid decisions rejected
- Parse all field types correctly
- Handle optional fields gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented with all fields
- [ ] Validation function catches all errors
- [ ] Example plans provided for testing
- [ ] Summary function works
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:01.268595285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented validate_review_plan(), summarize_review_plan(), and get_review_plan_json_summary() functions in ru script. Added 14 unit tests in scripts/test_unit_review_plan.sh. All tests pass."}
-{"id":"bd-ldyv","title":"Implement quality gates framework (tests/lint before push)","description":"Task: Implement Quality Gates Framework

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

Policy Configuration
--------------------
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:

REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS=true
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true

Gate Results in Plan
--------------------
Update plan with gate results:
{
  "git": {
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  }
}

Testing
-------
- Verify auto-detection works
- Verify configured commands run
- Verify failures block push
- Verify secret scan catches patterns
- Test with missing tools

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Test gate runs before push
- [ ] Lint gate runs if configured
- [ ] Secret scan warns on patterns
- [ ] Failures block push with message
- [ ] Per-repo policies work
- [ ] Auto-detection works for common projects","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.491001528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-cutq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:10:25.431274346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-ldyv","title":"Implement quality gates framework (tests/lint before push)","description":"Task: Implement Quality Gates Framework

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

Policy Configuration
--------------------
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:

REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS=true
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true

Gate Results in Plan
--------------------
Update plan with gate results:

  "git": {
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  }
}

Testing
-------
- Verify auto-detection works
- Verify configured commands run
- Verify failures block push
- Verify secret scan catches patterns
- Test with missing tools

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Test gate runs before push
- [ ] Lint gate runs if configured
- [ ] Secret scan warns on patterns
- [ ] Failures block push with message
- [ ] Per-repo policies work
- [ ] Auto-detection works for common projects","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:17:18.595030171-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.491001528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-cutq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:10:25.431274346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mcvj","title":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing","description":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing

Overview
--------
Security safeguards, analytics collection, and comprehensive testing
to ensure the review system is safe, measurable, and reliable.

Components
----------

7.1 Security: Command Validation
   Prevent dangerous commands:
   - Blocked list: sudo, rm -rf, eval
   - gh mutation blocking in Plan mode
   - Approval required for risky ops
   - Pre-exec hook validation

7.2 Security: Secret Scanning
   Detect secrets before push:
   - gitleaks integration
   - Regex fallback patterns
   - Block push on detection
   - Human review option

7.3 Metrics Collection
   Track review analytics:
   - Reviews per period
   - Issues resolved
   - Questions asked/answered
   - Time per repo
   - Decision breakdown

7.4 Decision Tracking
   Learn from past decisions:
   - Record decision patterns
   - Suggest based on history
   - Per-repo preferences
   - JSONL format for analysis

7.5 Analytics Dashboard
   Visualize review effectiveness:
   - Overview stats
   - Decision breakdown chart
   - Top repos by activity
   - Efficiency trend

7.6 Unit Tests
   Test individual functions:
   - GraphQL batching
   - Priority scoring
   - JSON parsing
   - State management

7.7 Integration Tests
   Test full workflows:
   - Discovery to apply
   - Question handling
   - Error recovery
   - Resume functionality

7.8 Test Fixtures
   Mock data for testing:
   - Claude stream examples
   - GraphQL responses
   - Review plan examples
   - Error scenarios

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Security controls prevent dangerous ops
- Metrics collected accurately
- Analytics dashboard works
- Unit test coverage adequate
- Integration tests pass
- Fixtures enable reliable testing

Estimated Effort
----------------
~500 lines (security/metrics)
~800 lines (tests)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:00.837840538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:00.837840538-05:00"}
 {"id":"bd-mnu9","title":"Add cmd_review() command skeleton with argument parsing","description":"# Task: Add cmd_review() Command Skeleton

## Purpose
Create the entry point for all review functionality - the cmd_review() function that will orchestrate the entire review workflow.

## Implementation Details

### Location in ru script
Add after cmd_prune() (around line 3634), following existing command pattern.

### Function Structure
```bash
cmd_review() {
    local mode="auto"
    local parallel=4
    local dry_run="false"
    local resume="false"
    local apply="false"
    local push="false"
    local priority_threshold="all"
    local max_repos=""
    local max_runtime=""
    local max_questions=""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_review_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_review_prerequisites || exit 3

    # Generate unique run ID
    REVIEW_RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"

    # Acquire global lock
    acquire_review_lock || { log_error "Another review is running"; exit 1; }

    # Auto-detect driver
    [[ "$mode" == "auto" ]] && mode=$(detect_review_driver)

    # Discovery phase
    log_step "Scanning repositories for open issues and PRs..."
    local -a work_items
    discover_work_items work_items "$priority_threshold" "$max_repos"

    if [[ ${#work_items[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No work items need review"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # Show summary
    show_discovery_summary "${work_items[@]}"

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Dry run - exiting without starting sessions"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # ... orchestration phases follow
}
```

### Arguments to Support
| Argument | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| --plan | (default) | Generate plans only, no mutations |
| --apply | false | Execute approved plans |
| --mode=MODE | auto | Driver: auto, ntm, local |
| --parallel=N | 4 | Concurrent sessions |
| --repos=PATTERN | all | Filter repos by pattern |

 {"id":"bd-p2ip","title":"Implement review lock mechanism (flock-based)","description":"# Task: Implement Review Lock Mechanism

## Purpose
Prevent concurrent `ru review` runs which would cause chaos with overlapping worktrees, duplicate API calls, and conflicting state updates.

## Implementation Details

### Lock File Location
```bash
REVIEW_LOCK_FILE="$RU_STATE_DIR/review.lock"
```

### acquire_review_lock()
```bash
acquire_review_lock() {
    mkdir -p "$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Open fd 200 for locking
    exec 200>"$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE"
    
    # Try non-blocking lock
    if ! flock -n 200; then
        # Check who holds it
        local holder_info=""
        if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
            holder_info=$(cat "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info")
        fi
        log_error "Another review session is active"
        [[ -n "$holder_info" ]] && log_error "Started: $holder_info"
        log_info "Use 'ru review --status' to check, or wait for completion"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Write lock info
    cat > "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" << EOF
{
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "pid": $$,
  "mode": "$mode"
}
EOF
    
    return 0
}
```

### release_review_lock()
```bash
release_review_lock() {
    # Remove info file
    rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
    
    # Release flock
    flock -u 200 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

### Trap Handler Integration
```bash
# In cmd_review(), set up trap
trap 'release_review_lock; exit 130' INT TERM

# Ensure cleanup on any exit
trap_cleanup_review() {
    release_review_lock
    cleanup_stale_worktrees
}
trap trap_cleanup_review EXIT
```

### Stale Lock Detection
```bash
check_stale_lock() {
    if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
        local lock_pid
        lock_pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" 2>/dev/null)
        
        # Check if process still exists
        if [[ -n "$lock_pid" ]] && ! kill -0 "$lock_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Found stale lock from dead process $lock_pid"
            rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
            return 0  # Lock is stale
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # Lock is valid or doesn't exist
}
```

## Why flock?
- Atomic lock acquisition
- Automatically released on process exit (even crash)
- No race conditions
- Works on Linux, macOS, BSD
- Already used successfully in parallel sync

## Edge Cases
- Process killed with SIGKILL: Lock released by kernel, but .info file remains
- Machine reboot: Lock file and .info persist but are stale
- Multiple users: Each user has own state dir, so no conflict

## Testing
- Verify lock prevents second ru review
- Verify lock released on normal exit
- Verify lock released on Ctrl+C
- Verify stale lock detected after kill -9
- Verify lock info shows useful details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only one ru review can run at a time
- [ ] Lock info shows run_id, start time, PID
- [ ] Lock released on all exit paths
- [ ] Stale locks from crashed processes handled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.209434378-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full flock-based review lock mechanism with JSON info file (run_id, started_at, pid, mode). Added check_stale_lock() for dead process detection. Proper trap handlers for INT/TERM/EXIT. All acceptance criteria met: single concurrent review, detailed lock info, cleanup on all exit paths, stale lock detection.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p2ip","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.831071716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-s3iy","title":"Implement non-interactive mode for CI/automation","description":"# Task: Implement Non-Interactive Mode

## Purpose
Add --non-interactive flag to `ru review` for CI/automation environments where no human is available to answer questions.

## Background
From AGENTS.md:
- Non-interactive mode (--non-interactive) suppresses prompts for CI/automation
- Must work in headless environments (no TTY)

## Non-Interactive Behavior

### Question Handling
When a question arises in non-interactive mode:

1. **AskUserQuestion with recommended option**: Auto-select recommended
2. **AskUserQuestion without recommended**: Log warning, skip item
3. **Agent text question**: Log warning, skip item
4. **External prompt (password, conflict)**: Fail with exit code 3

### Implementation

```bash
handle_question_non_interactive() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local session_id="$2"
    
    local reason recommended
    reason=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .reason)
    recommended=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .recommended // "")
    
    case "$reason" in
        ask_user_question)
            if [[ -n "$recommended" ]]; then
                log_info "[non-interactive] Auto-selecting: $recommended"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "$recommended"
                return 0
            else
                log_warn "[non-interactive] No recommended option, skipping item"
                record_item_outcome "$repo_id" "issue" "$number" "skipped" \
                    "Skipped in non-interactive mode (no recommended option)"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
                return 0
            fi
            ;;
        agent_question_text)
            log_warn "[non-interactive] Agent text question, skipping"
            driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
            return 0
            ;;
        external_prompt)
            log_error "[non-interactive] External prompt requires human - failing"
            return 3
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Auto-Answer Policy

Configurable via:
```bash
REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"  # auto|skip|fail
```

- **auto**: Use recommended if available, skip otherwise
- **skip**: Always skip questions (log them for later)
- **fail**: Fail immediately on any question (strictest)

### Question Logging

In non-interactive mode, log all questions for later review:
```bash
log_skipped_question() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/skipped-questions-$REVIEW_RUN_ID.json"
    
    echo "$question_info" >> "$log_file"
}
```

### TTY Detection

```bash
check_interactive_capability() {
    # Check if stdin is a terminal
    if [[ \! -t 0 ]]; then
        if [[ "$REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE" \!= "true" ]]; then
            log_warn "No TTY detected, enabling --non-interactive mode"
            REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE=true
        fi
    fi
}
```

### Summary Output

In non-interactive mode, output a summary of skipped questions:
```
Review completed with 3 skipped questions.
Questions logged to: ~/.local/state/ru/skipped-questions-20250104-103000.json

Use "ru review --resume --interactive" to answer remaining questions.
```

## Flags

```bash
--non-interactive     # Enable non-interactive mode
--auto-answer=POLICY  # Policy: auto (default), skip, fail
```

## Integration Points
- Detect non-interactive at startup
- Handle questions differently based on mode
- Log skipped questions for later
- Provide resume path for answering questions later

## Testing
- Verify TTY detection works
- Verify auto-answer selects recommended
- Verify skip logs questions
- Verify fail mode exits on question
- Verify resume path works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --non-interactive flag works
- [ ] TTY auto-detection works
- [ ] Auto-answer uses recommended options
- [ ] Skipped questions logged
- [ ] Clear summary output
- [ ] --resume allows answering skipped questions later

+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# REVIEW EXIT CODES + ERROR CLASSIFICATION (bd-jen3)
+#
+# Review uses ru's standard exit codes:
+#   0 success
+#   1 partial failure
+#   2 conflicts / manual intervention required
+#   3 dependency/system error
+#   4 invalid arguments
+#   5 interrupted (resume supported by checkpoint bead)
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+classify_review_error() {
+    local error_type="${1:-}"
+    local context="${2:-}"
+
+    case "$error_type" in
+        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
+            echo "partial"
+            ;;
+        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
+            echo "conflict"
+            ;;
+        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
+            echo "system"
+            ;;
+        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
+            echo "invalid"
+            ;;
+        interrupted|timeout|max_runtime|max_questions)
+            echo "interrupted"
+            ;;
+        *)
+            # Default to "partial" because review can often continue on other repos.
+            echo "unknown"
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+review_exit_code_for_classification() {
+    local classification="${1:-unknown}"
+    case "$classification" in
+        partial) echo 1 ;;
+        conflict) echo 2 ;;
+        system) echo 3 ;;
+        invalid) echo 4 ;;
+        interrupted) echo 5 ;;
+        *) echo 1 ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+review_exit_code_for_error() {
+    local error_type="${1:-}"
+    local context="${2:-}"
+
+    local classification
+    classification=$(classify_review_error "$error_type" "$context")
+    review_exit_code_for_classification "$classification"
+}
+
+aggregate_exit_code() {
+    local max_code=0
+    local code
+    for code in "$@"; do
+        [[ "$code" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || continue
+        (( code > max_code )) && max_code="$code"
+    done
+    echo "$max_code"
+}
+
+finalize_review_exit() {
+    local exit_code="$1"
+
+    case "$exit_code" in
+        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
+        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
+        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
+        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
+        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
+        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
+        *) log_error "Review failed (unknown exit code: $exit_code)" ;;
+    esac
+
+    exit "$exit_code"
+}
+

+#==============================================================================
+# SECTION 13.7: QUALITY GATES FRAMEWORK
+#==============================================================================
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# detect_test_command: Auto-detect the test command for a project
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Project directory path
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 if command detected, 1 if no test framework found
+#
+# Outputs:
+#   Test command to stdout
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+detect_test_command() {
+    local project_dir="$1"
+
+    # Check Makefile for test target
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$project_dir/Makefile" 2>/dev/null; then
+        echo "make test"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Check for package.json (npm/node)
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/package.json" ]]; then
+        if jq -e '.scripts.test' "$project_dir/package.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            echo "npm test"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check for Cargo.toml (Rust)
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
+        echo "cargo test"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Check for Python projects
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/pyproject.toml" ]] || [[ -f "$project_dir/setup.py" ]]; then
+        if [[ -d "$project_dir/tests" ]] || [[ -d "$project_dir/test" ]]; then
+            echo "pytest"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check for Go projects
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/go.mod" ]]; then
+        echo "go test ./..."
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Check for shell test scripts
+    if [[ -x "$project_dir/scripts/run_all_tests.sh" ]]; then
+        echo "./scripts/run_all_tests.sh"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# detect_lint_command: Auto-detect the lint command for a project
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Project directory path
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 if command detected, 1 if no linter found
+#
+# Outputs:
+#   Lint command to stdout
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+detect_lint_command() {
+    local project_dir="$1"
+
+    # Check for package.json lint script
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/package.json" ]]; then
+        if jq -e '.scripts.lint' "$project_dir/package.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            echo "npm run lint"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check for shell scripts (use shellcheck)
+    if command -v shellcheck &>/dev/null; then
+        local shell_scripts
+        shell_scripts=$(find "$project_dir" -maxdepth 2 -name "*.sh" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
+        if [[ -n "$shell_scripts" ]]; then
+            echo "shellcheck -S warning \$(find . -name '*.sh' -type f)"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check for Python (ruff or flake8)
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/pyproject.toml" ]] || [[ -f "$project_dir/setup.py" ]]; then
+        if command -v ruff &>/dev/null; then
+            echo "ruff check ."
+            return 0
+        elif command -v flake8 &>/dev/null; then
+            echo "flake8 ."
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check for Go
+    if [[ -f "$project_dir/go.mod" ]]; then
+        echo "go vet ./..."
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# run_test_gate: Run tests for a project
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Project directory path
+#   $2 - Optional: Test command override
+#   $3 - Optional: Timeout in seconds (default: 300)
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 on success, 1 on failure, 2 if no tests found
+#
+# Outputs:
+#   JSON result object to stdout
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+run_test_gate() {
+    local project_dir="$1"
+    local test_cmd="${2:-}"
+    local timeout="${3:-300}"
+    local start_time
+    local exit_code=0
+    local output=""
+
+    start_time=$(date +%s)
+
+    # Auto-detect if not provided
+    if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
+        test_cmd=$(detect_test_command "$project_dir") || {
+            jq -n '{ran: false, ok: null, reason: "no_tests_found"}'
+            return 2
+        }
+    fi
+
+    log_verbose "Running tests: $test_cmd"
+
+    # Run tests with timeout
+    if output=$(cd "$project_dir" && timeout "$timeout" bash -c "$test_cmd" 2>&1); then
+        exit_code=0
+    else
+        exit_code=$?
+    fi
+
+    local end_time duration
+    end_time=$(date +%s)
+    duration=$((end_time - start_time))
+
+    # Summarize output (last 10 lines or key metrics)
+    local output_summary
+    output_summary=$(echo "$output" | tail -10 | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-200)
+
+    jq -n \
+        --argjson ran true \
+        --argjson ok "$([ $exit_code -eq 0 ] && echo true || echo false)" \
+        --arg command "$test_cmd" \
+        --arg output_summary "$output_summary" \
+        --argjson duration_seconds "$duration" \
+        --argjson exit_code "$exit_code" \
+        '{
+            ran: $ran,
+            ok: $ok,
+            command: $command,
+            output_summary: $output_summary,
+            duration_seconds: $duration_seconds,
+            exit_code: $exit_code
+        }'
+
+    return $exit_code
+}
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# run_lint_gate: Run linting for a project
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Project directory path
+#   $2 - Optional: Lint command override
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 on success, 1 on failure, 2 if no linter found
+#
+# Outputs:
+#   JSON result object to stdout
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+run_lint_gate() {
+    local project_dir="$1"
+    local lint_cmd="${2:-}"
+    local exit_code=0
+    local output=""
+
+    # Auto-detect if not provided
+    if [[ -z "$lint_cmd" ]]; then
+        lint_cmd=$(detect_lint_command "$project_dir") || {
+            jq -n '{ran: false, ok: null, reason: "no_linter_found"}'
+            return 2
+        }
+    fi
+
+    log_verbose "Running lint: $lint_cmd"
+
+    # Run linter
+    if output=$(cd "$project_dir" && bash -c "$lint_cmd" 2>&1); then
+        exit_code=0
+    else
+        exit_code=$?
+    fi
+
+    local output_summary
+    output_summary=$(echo "$output" | head -20 | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-300)
+
+    jq -n \
+        --argjson ran true \
+        --argjson ok "$([ $exit_code -eq 0 ] && echo true || echo false)" \
+        --arg command "$lint_cmd" \
+        --arg output_summary "$output_summary" \
+        --argjson exit_code "$exit_code" \
+        '{
+            ran: $ran,
+            ok: $ok,
+            command: $command,
+            output_summary: $output_summary,
+            exit_code: $exit_code
+        }'
+
+    return $exit_code
+}
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# run_secret_scan: Scan for secrets in project changes
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Project directory path
+#   $2 - Optional: "staged" to scan only staged changes
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 on success (no secrets), 1 on failure (secrets found), 2 on warning
+#
+# Outputs:
+#   JSON result object to stdout
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+run_secret_scan() {
+    local project_dir="$1"
+    local scope="${2:-all}"
+    local findings=()
+    local exit_code=0
+
+    # Use gitleaks if available
+    if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
+        log_verbose "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
+        local gl_output
+        if ! gl_output=$(gitleaks detect --source "$project_dir" --no-git 2>&1); then
+            exit_code=1
+            # Include first line of gitleaks output in findings
+            local gl_summary
+            gl_summary=$(echo "$gl_output" | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
+            findings+=("gitleaks: ${gl_summary:-detected potential secrets}")
+        fi
+    else
+        # Regex fallback
+        log_verbose "Scanning for secrets with regex patterns"
+        local patterns=(
+            'password\s*[:=]'
+            'api.?key\s*[:=]'
+            'secret\s*[:=]'
+            'token\s*[:=]'
+            'AWS_ACCESS_KEY'
+            'AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY'
+            'PRIVATE_KEY'
+            'BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY'
+            'BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY'
+        )
+
+        local diff_output
+        if [[ "$scope" == "staged" ]]; then
+            diff_output=$(git -C "$project_dir" diff --cached 2>/dev/null || true)
+        else
+            diff_output=$(git -C "$project_dir" diff HEAD~1..HEAD 2>/dev/null || true)
+        fi
+
+        for pattern in "${patterns[@]}"; do
+            if echo "$diff_output" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
+                exit_code=2  # Warning
+                findings+=("Potential secret pattern: $pattern")
+            fi
+        done
+    fi
+
+    local findings_json="[]"
+    if [[ ${#findings[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        findings_json=$(printf '%s\n' "${findings[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .)
+    fi
+
+    jq -n \
+        --argjson ran true \
+        --argjson ok "$([ $exit_code -eq 0 ] && echo true || echo false)" \
+        --argjson warning "$([ $exit_code -eq 2 ] && echo true || echo false)" \
+        --argjson findings "$findings_json" \
+        --arg tool "$(command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null && echo gitleaks || echo regex)" \
+        '{
+            ran: $ran,
+            ok: $ok,
+            warning: $warning,
+            tool: $tool,
+            findings: $findings
+        }'
+
+    return $exit_code
+}
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# run_quality_gates: Run all quality gates for a project
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Project directory path (worktree)
+#   $2 - Path to review-plan.json file
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 on success, 1 on test/lint failure, 2 on secret warning
+#
+# Outputs:
+#   Combined JSON result to stdout
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+run_quality_gates() {
+    local wt_path="$1"
+    local plan_file="$2"
+    local repo_id=""
+    local overall_ok=true
+    local has_warning=false
+
+    # Get repo ID from plan
+    if [[ -f "$plan_file" ]]; then
+        repo_id=$(jq -r '.repo // ""' "$plan_file")
+    fi
+
+    # Load policy for this repo
+    local policy_json
+    policy_json=$(load_policy_for_repo "$repo_id")
+
+    local test_cmd lint_cmd
+    test_cmd=$(echo "$policy_json" | jq -r '.test_command // ""')
+    lint_cmd=$(echo "$policy_json" | jq -r '.lint_command // ""')
+
+    # Run lint gate
+    log_info "Running quality gates..."
+    local lint_result
+    lint_result=$(run_lint_gate "$wt_path" "$lint_cmd")
+    local lint_exit=$?
+
+    if [[ $lint_exit -eq 1 ]]; then
+        overall_ok=false
+        log_error "Lint gate failed"
+    elif [[ $lint_exit -eq 2 ]]; then
+        log_verbose "No linter configured"
+    else
+        log_info "Lint gate passed"
+    fi
+
+    # Run test gate
+    local test_result
+    test_result=$(run_test_gate "$wt_path" "$test_cmd")
+    local test_exit=$?
+
+    if [[ $test_exit -eq 1 ]]; then
+        overall_ok=false
+        log_error "Test gate failed"
+    elif [[ $test_exit -eq 2 ]]; then
+        log_verbose "No tests configured"
+    else
+        log_info "Test gate passed"
+    fi
+
+    # Run secret scan
+    local secret_result
+    secret_result=$(run_secret_scan "$wt_path")
+    local secret_exit=$?
+
+    if [[ $secret_exit -eq 1 ]]; then
+        overall_ok=false
+        log_error "Secret scan failed - secrets detected"
+    elif [[ $secret_exit -eq 2 ]]; then
+        has_warning=true
+        log_warn "Secret scan warning - potential secrets detected"
+    else
+        log_info "Secret scan passed"
+    fi
+
+    # Combine results
+    jq -n \
+        --argjson tests "$test_result" \
+        --argjson lint "$lint_result" \
+        --argjson secrets "$secret_result" \
+        --argjson overall_ok "$overall_ok" \
+        --argjson has_warning "$has_warning" \
+        '{
+            overall_ok: $overall_ok,
+            has_warning: $has_warning,
+            tests: $tests,
+            lint: $lint,
+            secrets: $secrets
+        }'
+
+    if [[ "$overall_ok" == "false" ]]; then
+        return 1
+    elif [[ "$has_warning" == "true" ]]; then
+        return 2
+    fi
+    return 0
+}
+
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# update_plan_with_gates: Update review plan with quality gate results
+#
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Path to review-plan.json file
+#   $2 - Quality gates result JSON
+#
+# Returns:
+#   0 on success, 1 on failure
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+update_plan_with_gates() {
+    local plan_file="$1"
+    local gates_result="$2"
+
+    if [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]]; then
+        log_error "Plan file not found: $plan_file"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Merge gates result into plan's git section
+    local updated_plan
+    updated_plan=$(jq --argjson gates "$gates_result" '
+        .git = (.git // {}) |
+        .git.tests = $gates.tests |
+        .git.lint = $gates.lint |
+        .git.secrets = $gates.secrets |
+        .git.quality_gates_ok = $gates.overall_ok |
+        .git.quality_gates_warning = $gates.has_warning
+    ' "$plan_file")
+
+    echo "$updated_plan" > "$plan_file"
+    return 0
+}
+
 #==============================================================================
 # SECTION 14: MAIN DISPATCH
 #==============================================================================
